hero.title: Donald Smith Ogono
hero.subtitle: Red Team Tool Developer | Cybersecurity Researcher
hero.description: Low-level offensive security researcher specializing in EDR evasion, syscall-level exploitation, and custom red team tooling.

about.text: Donald is a low-level offensive security researcher specializing in EDR evasion, syscall-level exploitation, and custom red team tooling. His work bridges C/C++ systems programming, Windows internals, adversarial simulation, and AI-assisted exploit development.

projects.name: Project Charon
projects.description: Custom artifact builder and EDR bypass framework.
projects.details: A sophisticated framework designed to bypass modern EDR solutions using advanced injection and obfuscation techniques.

projects.name: Red Team Lab
projects.description: Adversarial emulation and persistence research lab.
projects.details: A controlled environment for testing persistent beacons and local privilege escalation techniques.

projects.name: Sysmon Home Defense
projects.details: A telemetry system optimized for high-signal threat hunting with significantly reduced false positives.

principles.name: Adversarial Thinking
principles.description: Understanding the attacker's mindset is the only way to build truly resilient systems. I don't just look for bugs; I look for paths of least resistance.

principles.name: Red/Blue Feedback Loop
principles.description: Offensive research should always inform defensive posture. Every exploit I develop is a lesson in how to improve detection and mitigation.

principles.name: Responsible Disclosure
principles.description: Innovation in exploit development must be balanced with ethical responsibility. My goal is a more secure ecosystem through rigorous testing and coordinated disclosure.
